LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT, AMY MEHTA, MD'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO KERN COUNTY IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY. THE WITNESSES AND RECORDS PERTAINING TO PLAINTIFF MONA FLORES' CLAIMS ARE ALL CENTERED IN BAKERSFIELD, LOCATED IN KERN COUNTY. THAT IS WHERE JUAN FLORES RECEIVED HIS CARE AND TREATMENT WHICH IS THE BASIS FOR THIS LAWSUIT. DR. MEHTA HAS ESTABLISHED THAT ALL DOCTORS AND MEDICAL PERSONNEL INVOLVED IN THE TREATMENT, CARE, AND SUPERVISION OF MR. FLORES ARE LOCATED IN KERN COUNTY. DR. MEHTA'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. AS DEMONSTRATED BY THE MAP PROVIDED BY DR. MEHTA, ALL WITNESSES WILL BE FAR LESS INCONVENIENCED BY A TRIAL IN KERN COUNTY THAN IN SAN FRANCISCO. MS. FLORES IDENTIFIED HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY PERCIPIENT WITNESSES WHO LIVE OR WORK IN THE BAY AREA, MUCH LESS SAN FRANCISCO. WHILE DR. MEHTA DID NOT PRESENT ANY DECLARATIONS FROM NONPARTY WITNESSES ATTESTING TO THE INCONVENIENCE OF TRAVELLING FROM KERN COUNTY TO SAN FRANCISCO, CASE LAW DOES NOT REQUIRE HER TO DO SO WHERE, AS HERE, THE INCONVENIENCE CAN BE REASONABLY PRESUMED FROM THE DISTANCES INVOLVED. (PEARSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1962) 199 CAL.APP.2D 69, 78 ("IT IS A MOST REASONABLE AND NATURAL INFERENCE" TO PRESUME INCONVENIENCE BASED ON DISTANCE ALONE).) DR. MEHTA MUST PAY ALL TRANSFER COSTS AND MUST DO SO WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS OF THIS ORDER. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Off Calendar

Joinder Of Defendants Dignity Health And Dennis Sumenig, R.N. In Motion To Transfer Venue Filed By Defendant Amy Mehta M.D.
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Devon R. McTeer of the law offices of McCormick Barstow LLP (559-433-1300) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH, DENNIS SUMENIG's Joinder Of Defendants Dignity Health And Dennis Sumenig, R.N. In Motion To Transfer Venue Filed By Defendant Amy Mehta M.D. is Off calendar in light of the tentative ruling on the motion sought to be joined.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Devon R. McTeer of the law offices of McCormick Barstow LLP (559-433-1300) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT, AMY MEHTA, MD's Motion To Transfer Venue to Kern County is GRANTED. This case is transferred to Kern County. The witnesses and records pertaining to plaintiff Mona Flores' claims are all centered in Bakersfield, located in Kern County. That is where Juan Flores received his care and treatment which is the basis for this lawsuit. Dr. Mehta has established that all doctors and medical personnel involved in the treatment, care, and supervision of Mr. Flores are located in Kern County. Dr. Mehta's request for judicial notice is unopposed and granted. As demonstrated by the map provided by Dr. Mehta, all witnesses will be far less inconvenienced by a trial in Kern County than in San Francisco. Ms. Flores identified has not identified any percipient witnesses who live or work in the Bay Area, much less San Francisco. While Dr. Mehta did not present any declarations from nonparty witnesses attesting to the inconvenience of travelling from Kern County to San Francisco, case law does not require her to do so where, as here, the inconvenience can be reasonably presumed from the distances involved. (Pearson v. Superior Court (1962) 199 Cal.App.2d 69, 78 ("it is a most reasonable and natural inference" to presume inconvenience based on distance alone).) Dr. Mehta must pay all transfer costs and must do so within fifteen days of this order. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Continued To Sep-27-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
